header.name: Banza-Signature
header.note: This is the canonical protocol-level constant. MUST match exactly (case-insensitive on lookup, title-case in canonical form). Any SDK using a different header name is non-conformant.

t.description: Unix timestamp (seconds) when the webhook was dispatched by the protocol.

v1.description: HMAC-SHA256 digest of the signed payload, lowercase hex-encoded (64 characters).
v1.note: The 'v1' prefix is versioned. Verifiers MUST check v1 and MAY ignore unknown prefix fields (e.g. v2 in future). Never fail on the presence of unknown fields.

algorithm.name: HMAC-SHA256

signed_payload_construction.note: raw_body_bytes MUST be the unmodified HTTP request body as received on the wire — before any JSON parsing, whitespace normalization, or re-encoding. Any alteration to the byte sequence invalidates the signature.

algorithm.reference_implementation_go:
mac := hmac.New(sha256.New, []byte(secret))
fmt.Fprintf(mac, "%d.", ts)
mac.Write(rawBody)
v1 := hex.EncodeToString(mac.Sum(nil))

steps.name: Parse header
steps.description: Split the Banza-Signature header value on ',' and extract 't' and 'v1' fields. If either is absent or malformed, reject with HTTP 400.

steps.name: Replay protection
steps.description: Compute age = abs(current_unix_seconds - t). If age > tolerance_seconds, reject with HTTP 400. This prevents an attacker from capturing a legitimate webhook and re-delivering it later.

steps.name: Constant-time comparison
steps.description: Compare expected_hex to the v1 value from the header using a constant-time comparison function. Variable-time comparison allows timing oracle attacks that can recover the secret. Reject with HTTP 401 on mismatch.

steps.name: Parse event
steps.description: Only after successful verification, parse the JSON body. Return HTTP 200 to acknowledge receipt.

tolerance.description: Maximum age of an incoming webhook request. Requests where |now - t| > 300 MUST be rejected. Set to 0 to disable replay protection (not recommended in production).
tolerance.note: Both past and future timestamps are checked — a timestamp 500 seconds in the future is also rejected.

test_vectors._description: All vectors use the same shared secret and timestamp. Use to validate SDK implementations.

vectors.title: Future timestamp — verification MUST fail (current_time = 1716000000, t = 1716000500)
Malformed header — missing t= and v1= — MUST raise parse error
vectors.note: Must fail before any HMAC computation.

known_implementation_discrepancies.description: The Python SDK uses the wrong header name. Any Python integration reading 'Banzami-Signature' will silently fail to find the header when the protocol sends 'Banza-Signature'. Must be corrected before the Python SDK is marked production-ready.

sdk_conformance_requirements: Use header name 'Banza-Signature' (canonical) with case-insensitive lookup
Parse both 't' and 'v1' fields from comma-separated header value
Reject requests where abs(now - t) > 300 seconds
Use HMAC-SHA256 with UTF-8 encoded key and payload
Read raw body bytes before any JSON parsing
Expose constructEvent(rawBody, header, secret) -> WebhookEvent as primary API
Expose generateTestSignature(rawBody, secret, timestamp?) -> string for test tooling

common_mistakes.mistake: Signing only the body without timestamp
